Given a vector field $${\overrightarrow F ,}$$ the divergence theorem states that

The directional derivative of $$f\left( {x,y} \right) = 2{x^2} + 3{y^2} + {z^2}\,\,$$ at point $$P\left( {2,1,3} \right)\,\,$$ in the direction of the vector $$\,\,a = \overrightarrow i  - 2\overrightarrow k \,\,$$  is

Let $a_R$ be the unit radial vector in the spherical co-ordinate system. For which of the following value(s) of $n$, the divergence of the radial vector field $f(R)=a_R \frac{1}{R^n}$ is independent of $R$ ?
explanation:
$$ \begin{aligned}Given : \bar{f}(R) & =\frac{1}{R^n} \hat{a}_R \\ \nabla \cdot \bar{f} & =\frac{1}{R^2 \sin \theta}\left[\frac{\partial}{\partial R}\left(R^2 \sin \theta \cdot \frac{1}{R^n}\right)\right] \\ & =\frac{1}{R^2}\left[\frac{\partial}{\partial R}\left(R^{2-n}\right)\right] \\ & =\frac{(2-n) R^{-n+1}}{R^2}=(2-n) R^{-n-1} \end{aligned} $$

For $\nabla . \bar{f}$ to be independent of $R$,

Case (i) :

$$ \begin{aligned} -n-1 & =0 \\\Rightarrow -n & =1 \quad \quad\Rightarrow n=-1 \end{aligned} $$

Case (ii) :

$$ 2-n=0 \Rightarrow n=2 $$

content: Consider a vector $\vec{u} = 2\hat{x} + \hat{y} + 2\hat{z}$, where $\hat{x}$, $\hat{y}$, $\hat{z}$ represent unit vectors along the coordinate axes $x$, $y$, $z$ respectively. The directional derivative of the function $f(x, y, z) = 2\ln(xy) + \ln(yz) + 3\ln(xz)$ at the point $(x, y, z) = (1, 1, 1)$ in the direction of $\vec{u}$ is
Let $$f(x,y,z) = 4{x^2} + 7xy + 3x{z^2}$$. The direction in which the function f(x, y, z) increases most rapidly at point P = (1, 0, 2) is
explanation:
Scalar function f(x, y, z) rapidly increases in the direction of its normal vector at P.

$$\therefore$$ $$\overline a = \nabla f$$

$$ = \left( {i{\partial \over {\partial x}} + j{\partial \over {\partial y}} + k{\partial \over {\partial z}}} \right)f$$

$$ = i(8x + 7y + 3{z^2}) + j(7x) + k(6xz)$$

$${(\nabla f)_{(1,0,2)}} = 20i + 7j + 12k$$

content: Let $$\overrightarrow E (x,y,z) = 2{x^2}\widehat i + 5y\widehat j + 3z\widehat k$$. The value of $$\mathop{\int\!\!\!\int\!\!\!\int}\limits_{\kern-5.5pt V} {(\overrightarrow \nabla \,.\,\overrightarrow E )dV} $$, where V is the volume enclosed by the unit cube defined by 0 $$\le$$ x $$\le$$ 1, 0 $$\le$$ y $$\le$$ 1, and 0 $$\le$$ z $$\le$$ 1, is
explanation:
$$\overrightarrow \nabla \,.\,\overrightarrow E = \left( {i{\partial \over {\partial x}} + j{\partial \over {\partial y}} + z{\partial \over {\partial z}}} \right)\,.\,\left( {2{x^2}i + 5yj + 3zk} \right)$$

$$ = 4x + 5 + 3$$

$$\therefore$$ $$\mathop{\int\!\!\!\int\!\!\!\int}\limits_{\kern-5.5pt V} {\overrightarrow \nabla \,.\,\overrightarrow E dV = \int\limits_{x = 0}^1 {\int\limits_{y = 0}^1 {\int\limits_{z = 0}^1 {(4x + 5 + 3)dzdydx} } } } $$

$$ = \int\limits_0^1 {\int\limits_0^1 {(4x + 5 + 3)dzdydx} } $$

$$ = \int\limits_0^1 {\int\limits_0^1 {(4xz + 8z)_0^1dydx} } $$

$$ = \int\limits_0^1 {(4xy + 8y)_0^1dx} $$

$$ = (2{x^2} + 8x)_0^1$$

$$ = 10$$

content: The line integral of the vector field $$\,\,F = 5xz\widehat i + \left( {3{x^2} + 2y} \right)\widehat j + {x^2}z\widehat k\,\,$$  along a path from $$(0, 0, 0)$$ to $$(1,1,1)$$ parameterized by $$\left( {t,{t^2},t} \right)$$  is _________.
